Black-zone / missed signal: Access barriers, informal/private care, persistent symptoms or high-risk history never enter stable diagnostic pathway.
Product prevention: Patient-held record import; access-barrier fields; CHW/informal referral capture; incomplete-workup registry.
Safety guardrail: Provenance labels; offline/SMS/paper mode; clinician verification.

Black-zone / missed signal: Repeat visit, family history, referral note or deterioration not visible before triage.
Product prevention: Front-door repeat-visit banner; referral-note visibility; arrival-to-assessment timer; family/caregiver concern field.
Safety guardrail: Avoid algorithmic downgrades; registration escalation button.

Black-zone / missed signal: Single-symptom routing loses complexity, prior cancer/high-risk history, or patient meaning.
Product prevention: Hybrid intake: multi-symptom, free text, prior-history auto-pull, “not captured” and human-review escape hatch.
Safety guardrail: Accessibility/language fallback; emergency warning; human override.

Black-zone / missed signal: Abnormal vitals, repeat visits and red flags lose salience under cognitive strain.
Product prevention: Triage co-pilot, repeat-vital timers, deterioration check-in, acuity plus diagnostic-uncertainty view.
Safety guardrail: Support nurse judgment; calibrated timers; monitor over-alerting.

Black-zone / missed signal: First plausible diagnosis or mental-health/age/gender label closes inquiry.
Product prevention: Diagnostic time-out: dangerous-to-miss alternatives, bias/misattribution guardrail, patient quote visible.
Safety guardrail: Do not accuse; require clinician-reviewed red-flag check only when signals are discordant.

Black-zone / missed signal: Family history, medication access, patient’s own theory, functional impact and prior test context are not integrated.
Product prevention: Risk-context checklist and structured timeline with patient/caregiver input.

Black-zone / missed signal: Time-sensitive exams absent or delayed; telehealth gap hides required in-person exam.
Product prevention: Complaint-linked exam checklist; telehealth-to-in-person conversion criteria; timer for torsion/appendicitis-like pathways.

Black-zone / missed signal: Wrong test, no test, stockout, or wrong imaging scope.
Product prevention: Order-set suggestions; imaging-scope warning; unavailable-test alternate plan; resource availability capture.
Safety guardrail: Do not force testing; adapt to local resources.

Black-zone / missed signal: Abnormal result exists but is not communicated, acknowledged or acted on.
Product prevention: Result/finding lifecycle tracker, owner/deadline, critical semantics, patient notification status.
Safety guardrail: Risk-ranked worklist and backup owner; explanation path for patient release.

Black-zone / missed signal: Uncertainty, pending tests and unresolved symptoms disappear from patient plan.
Product prevention: Concern-by-concern closure; “what remains unexplained”; pending-test contract; specific safety-net.
Safety guardrail: Avoid panic; plain language and clear contact routes.

Black-zone / missed signal: Referral delayed, wrong, rejected, advice-only or never completed.
Product prevention: Referral status taxonomy and completion tracker with next owner.
Safety guardrail: Distinguish administrative status from diagnostic conclusion.

Black-zone / missed signal: Return visits do not cause diagnostic reset.
Product prevention: Repeat-visit diagnostic reset and symptom-still-present check-ins.
Safety guardrail: Thresholds by setting; do not punish returns.

Black-zone / missed signal: Specialist sees one slice, wrong chart, remote note, or wrong specialty.
Product prevention: Cross-specialty summary pane, identity verification, clinical question and unresolved-pattern map.

Black-zone / missed signal: Slow trends and chronic invalidation hide deterioration.
Product prevention: Trend explorer, functional status, quality-of-life, unresolved-problem registry.
Safety guardrail: Clinical validation before alerts; avoid anxiety loops.
